role: Senior Data Analyst & Analytics Engineer - Franchise

evidence.domain_risk: Code complex business logic (royalties, taxable turnover, margins)
evidence.data_team_maturity: Build data marts and business layers using dbt on Databricks
evidence.jd_authorship: Code complex business logic (royalties, taxable turnover, margins)
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Support franchise partners' decisions regarding stock, supply chain, and pricing
evidence.autonomy_level: Support franchise partners' decisions regarding stock, supply chain, and pricing
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Build data marts and business layers using dbt on Databricks; Design and maintain dashboards; Adapt existing dashboards from other business domains

velocity_vs_rigour_reasoning: The JD emphasizes governance and control (row-level security, documentation of KPIs and business rules) with no corresponding velocity signals like 'fast-paced' or 'rapid delivery' in the responsibilities section.
domain_risk_quote: Code complex business logic (royalties, taxable turnover, margins)
domain_risk_reasoning: Errors in royalty calculations, taxable turnover, and margin logic directly affect partner financial reporting and tax compliance, making this financial data work with immediate consequences.
data_team_maturity_quote: Build data marts and business layers using dbt on Databricks
data_team_maturity_reasoning: Named tools in responsibilities with specific application context (building data marts), combined with the ability to adapt existing dashboards, indicates an established data infrastructure at mid-stage growth rather than early-stage or mature.
jd_authorship_quote: Build data marts and business layers using dbt on Databricks; Code complex business logic (royalties, taxable turnover, margins)
jd_authorship_reasoning: Technical specificity throughout (named tools, specific business logic, precise SQL/Tableau skills) indicates hiring manager who understands the technical work required.
stakeholder_orientation_quote: Support franchise partners' decisions regarding stock, supply chain, and pricing
stakeholder_orientation_reasoning: The primary responsibility is serving franchise partners' operational and commercial decisions, making external franchisees the dominant stakeholder audience.
autonomy_level_quote: Support franchise partners' decisions regarding stock, supply chain, and pricing
autonomy_level_reasoning: While the role owns technical implementation (build, design, implement), the overarching framing emphasizes supporting franchise partner priorities rather than setting independent analytics strategy.
greenfield_vs_fix_quote: Build data marts and business layers using dbt on Databricks; Design and maintain dashboards; Adapt existing dashboards from other business domains
ai_role_quote: Build data marts and business layers using dbt on Databricks
ai_role_reasoning: No AI tool usage, infrastructure for AI consumption, or AI-first expectations; standard analytics engineering work with dbt.
testing_framing_quote: No testing-related signal in provided phrases
testing_framing_reasoning: No mention of testing tools, quality ownership, or data contracts.
loss_aversion_framing_quote: Implement row-level security; Support franchise partners' decisions regarding stock, supply chain, and pricing
loss_aversion_framing_reasoning: Security requirement and emphasis on data supporting critical franchise business decisions signal operational trust/reliability concerns, but lack explicit regulatory or audit framing.
